_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Laparoscopy for Ectopic/ other benign disorders
procedure_name: Laparoscopy for Ectopic/ other benign disorders
source_files: Laparoscopy for Ectopic other benign disorders_Medco.pdf
Laparoscopy for Ectopic other benign disorders_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: MS/MD/DNB/DGO/ Equivalent (OB&GYN) with experience/training in endoscopy
clinical_indications: Ectopic pregnancy: Confirmed tubal pregnancy measuring more than 35 mm
Ectopic pregnancy: Concurrent intrauterine pregnancy ruled out through Serum HCG levels
Ectopic pregnancy: Failure of medical management of ectopic pregnancy
Peritubal adhesions/peritoneal adhesions (formation of bands of fibrous tissue joining intra-abdominal organs or parietal peritoneal surfaces after trauma, ischemia, infection, or irritative insult)
Benign ovarian tumors/ovarian cyst (common cause of pelvic masses in women, fertile age, after ruling out malignancy using Doppler USG, CA-125, CA 19-9)

Endometriosis (estrogen-dependent chronic condition with ectopic implantation of functional tissue lining the uterus outside uterine cavity, common clinical symptoms being pelvic pain and infertility, formation of chocolate cysts)

Pelvic Inflammatory Disease (Pyosalpinx/hydrosalpinx) (upper genital infections like endometritis, salpingitis, tubo-ovarian complex, tubo-ovarian abscess, diagnosed clinically with pelvic pain, fever, foul discharge, hyperleukocytosis, elevated CRP)
Tuboplasty / Reanastomosis (surgical repair of fallopian tubes damaged intentionally for sterilization or unintentionally by previous infections, surgery, or scar tissue produced by endometriosis)

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission
Serum Beta human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) levels (in case of ectopic pregnancy)
USG/CT/MRI (Abdomen/pelvis)/ / HSG (Hysterosalpingography) confirming presence of ectopic/other benign disorders/ pelvic lesions

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers clearly indicating the need for performing the surgery
Discharge summary with follow up advice
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical presentation, severity, imaging, and laboratory investigations, failure of medical management indicative of surgery (for Ectopic Pregnancy)?
Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of tubal surgery other than Ectopic pregnancy?
Did the clinical presentation, evaluation findings, and imaging/investigations confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed operative notes available with indications for and outcomes of the procedure?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, severity, imaging, or laboratory investigations for Ectopic Pregnancy not indicative of surgery, or medical management not failed
Clinical presentation or imaging not indicative of tubal surgery (other than Ectopic pregnancy)
Serum Beta human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) levels missing or inconsistent in cases of ectopic pregnancy
Tumor markers (CEA-125, etc.) missing or inconsistent in cases of suspected ovarian tumors/cysts
Imaging report (USG/CT/MRI/HSG) not confirming ectopic/other benign disorders or pelvic lesions
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination is missing at claim stage

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, availability of anesthesia and anesthetist, availability of laparoscopic equipment. For indication (1) secondary level facility is required with adequate endoscopy/laparoscopy facility. For indication (2) secondary or tertiary level facility depending upon the severity of the disease condition, is required with adequate endoscopy/laparoscopy facility. For indications (3-6) tertiary level facility is required.
clinical_key_pointers: Common presenting complaints: Amenorrhea (in case of ectopic pregnancy)
Common presenting complaints: Spotting/Bleeding per vagina
Common presenting complaints: Pain abdomen: esp. one sided, continuous/ intermittent
Common presenting complaints: Pyrexia & foul discharge (PID)
Past history of: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease
Past history of: Benign ovarian tumors
Past history of: Severe congestive dysmenorrhea (in endometriosis)
